remarks, offered soldiers testifying government Monday Tuesday, could pose high hurdle defense lawyers have indicated that Bales' mental health will part their case.
Testimony continues with several more witnesses Wednesday preliminary hearing that being held help determine whether case goes court-martial.
Bales, 39-year-old father from Lake Tapps, Wash., faces counts premeditated murder counts attempted murder March attack villages Balandi Alkozai, which counted nine children among victims.
prosecutor, Col. Morse, said Bales spent evening before massacre remote outpost Camp Belambay with other soldiers, watching movie about revenge killings, sharing contraband whiskey from plastic bottle discussing attack that cost their comrades leg.
Within hours, cape-wearing Bales slipped away from post embarked killing spree own, said prosecutor, Morse.
attacked village then returned Belambay, where woke colleague reported what he'd done, Morse said.
colleague testified that didn't believe Bales went back sleep.

medic, Sgt.

asked where came from where he'd been.

other soldiers, Pfcs.

Blodgett said reported operations center base, specialist told them monitor know came toward them.
shooting lasted minutes, Blodgett said.

other witnesses said that later, interpreter arrived with Afghan National Army soldiers reported that they seen American come from base.
Guinn gave slightly different account when recalled that interpreter said soldiers seen Americans arrive base, head back out.
After shootings, some Afghan villagers questioned whether they could have been carried soldier.

general, Boston's performance standardized tests tracked national trends since early 2000s, Boston made faster progress when nation whole improving, Boston's slowdown past five years been more pronounced.
Furthermore, while Boston continues outperform many other large urban districts, some peer cities have instituted reforms...that have contributed more rapid progress recent years compared BPS.
Meanwhile, struggled make dent persistent racial ethnic disparities test scores graduation rates.
Without launch innovative initiatives improve equity address stagnating achievement trends, could risk losing status national leader pre-K-12 education.
Speaking personally, found writing report sobering exercise.
certainly busy over last years including changes teacher hiring practices, expansion pre-K, change funds schools, among other things student achievement scores didn't budge.
Worse, some initiatives, like re-designed school assignment system, increased racial segregation have contributed declines achievement black Hispanic students.
Meanwhile, other efforts, such plans deal with Boston's aging school facilities create unified school application process, have struggled ground amidst political battles public pushback.
Boston's education system unique particulars, broader story similar what's going rest country.
Boston, like rest country, inflection point.
Boston currently searching fifth superintendent years, will critical that leader articulate clear vision forward.
While don't claim have answers, hope report useful leaders Boston elsewhere diagnose current trends give some historical explanations what happened why.
(You also watch live discussion this report here.)
Like Boston, country becoming more diverse, future gains will depend well schools able provide educational services most disadvantaged students.
This entry posted Early Childhood Education, Education Policy, Equity tagged Boston Public Schools, NAEP, urban education March 2019 Chad Aldeman.
What Good Higher Graduation Rates Students Aren't Learning More?
Thursday, National Assessment Educational Progress (NAEP) released results 2015 science assessment America's 4th, 8th, 12th grade students.
Only percent 12th graders scored above proficient level, compared percent graders percent graders.
while graders both small significant improvement from 2009, high school seniors stagnated earning same average score 2009 sample.
This also true across subgroups.
Among students colors, students with disabilities, English language learners (ELLs), rural students, female students, single group statistically significant score change from 2009.
similar trend April, when NAEP released 12th grade results 2015 reading math assessments.
Seniors' average reading score significantly change again across every single subgroup.
average 12th grade math score declined.
yet, earlier this month, data released U.S. Department Education (ED) showed that America's high school graduation rate reached record high percent, continuing five year trend.
stark contrast with this year's NAEP data, rates among students color, students with disabilities, ELLs, low-income students have improved.
While this certainly good news, begs question: What good higher graduation rates students aren't learning more?
According Secretary John King: "Students have high school diploma better 21st Century economy than students don't.
having higher graduation rate meaningful progress."
While high school graduates earn more than non-graduates, this answer still deeply unsatisfying.
States will have opportunity seriously address America's stagnant high schools coming years.
Every Student Succeeds (ESSA), signed into last December, provides greater flexibility states almost every facet federal K-12 education policy.
makes easier states spend Title money high school students.
also gives states much greater leeway using school improvement funds, including optional set-aside programs like Advanced Placement, International Baccalaureate, career technical education.
remains seen exactly states will implement law, luckily we'll have NAEP along give national snapshot student learning.
We're doing better shepherding students high school completion just need make sure they actually learn something.
This entry posted Accountability, Education Policy, Equity, Federal Education Policy, School Funding, State Education Policy, Student Assessment tagged high school, High school accountability, High school graduation rates, John King, NAEP, Science, Secondary Education, testing October 2016 Phillip Burgoyne-Allen.
